w:r.w:t: Everything a new chat needs to continue building carousels from Day 43 onwards.
Aspiring Full Stack / AI Developer. Documents learning publicly on Instagram.
HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Git, Firebase, OOP, ReactJS, Python, NodeJS
A repeatable, fast system for generating polished Instagram carousels that document Abdullah's #100DaysOfCode journey. Claude's job is to:
Analyse the day's learning material (lesson screenshots + GitHub code)
Decide how to frame it — as an informational educational post, not just a learning log
Write all slide content, keeping it concise and social-media appropriate
Generate a fully self-contained HTML file the user downloads and exports
— one post per day, documenting what was learned and built. Content from lesson screenshots and GitHub code.
— occasional multi-day synthesis. One clean educational carousel on a single concept (e.g. 'DOM Manipulation explained'). Designed for reach and shareability.

turn learning into teaching. Reframe what was learned that day as something useful for the reader. See Day 40 example: 'Building a Google Keep Clone' with 'Key Concept: map() + innerHTML' instead of just listing topics covered.
Orange accent — progress bar fill, tags, icons, inline code on light slides, CTA badge
Light orange — inline code on dark slides, icon tints, dark slide tag labels
Deep orange — CTA button text, callout box left-border on light slides
Warm off-white — light slide backgrounds (never pure white)
Dividers and row borders on light slides
Near-black with warm tint — dark slide backgrounds
Body text color on light slides
HTML page background color (outside the carousel)
Large, bold, quirky. The signature visual. -1.2px tracking on big headings.
Used for all method/function/property mentions in prose.
Used inside carbon-style editor blocks. Line-height 1.7.
Handle and day badge at bottom of each slide.
Present continuous tense for slide titles:
"What I'm Building", "What I'm Practising" — NEVER past tense ("What I Built")
All method/function/CSS class mentions in prose MUST be styled as inline code spans — e.g.
Concise content. This is social media, not a tutorial. Every word must earn its place.
Professional but smart, modern, and a little playful.
Captions follow a consistent format: Day X of #100DaysOfCode [emoji] [1-sentence hook]. Swipe to see how it works 👉
4. HTML File Architecture — Every Technical Detail
padding-top: 125% sizer div (NOT aspect-ratio CSS property — causes blank space in html2canvas)
Each slide = .slide wrapper > .slide-sizer (sets height) + .slide-inner (absolute fill, contains all content)
28px top, 30px left/right, 56px bottom (bottom clears the progress bar)
Set on BOTH .slide-sizer and .slide-inner so the sizer div matches
This is the most important technical detail. Do NOT use
on the viewport — it causes extra blank space in downloaded PNGs because html2canvas picks up offsetWidth/offsetHeight with subpixel bleed. Instead use:
/* Sizer div — sets exact 4:5 height via padding-top trick */
/* Inner div — fills sizer absolutely, holds all slide content */
After html2canvas, draw onto a second canvas sized exactly w*3 × h*3 to hard-trim any bleed
300ms between slides so browser doesn't block downloads
150ms after moving track before capturing, so the browser has time to paint
track.style.transition = 'none' during export loop, restored after
// 1. Move track to slide i (no animation)
await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 150)); // wait for paint
width: w, height: h, scrollX: 0, scrollY: 0,
Swipe/drag (pointer events) + dot indicators below the carousel
Left/right nav buttons have been removed. Dots only.
6px circles, orange active dot stretches to 16px pill
pointerdown/pointermove/pointerup on .vp — threshold 50px to trigger slide change
Hook — Day number, big topic title, profile pic, logos, #100DaysOfCode badge. No footer.
4 feature rows with SVG icons. Short subtitle. Footer at bottom.
Carbon-style code block + orange callout box explanation. Footer.
Carbon-style code block + amber callout box explanation. Footer.
"How [X] Works" or "N Ways I Used X"
Numbered steps (01/02/03) with inline code spans throughout. Footer.
4 challenge rows with SVG icons + inline code spans. Footer.
Profile pic, handle, day badge, tagline, CTA button. NO swipe arrow. Full progress bar.
Slide count is flexible (2–10). 7 is ideal for a full concept post. Adjust based on how much was learned that day.
Top row: profile pic (36px circle, dashed orange border for placeholder) + orange #100DaysOfCode pill + Google Keep logo + JS logo (top-right, 26px rounded squares)
Middle (flex:1, centered): DAY XX tag (10px, orange, 2px letter-spacing, caps) → big Bricolage Grotesque heading (38px, -1.2px tracking) → subtitle line in muted gray
Bottom row: @uncledullaz handle in 10px all-caps muted gray
All image slots are dashed orange placeholder divs. User replaces via
Position: absolute bottom, full width, z-index 10
Counter label: "3/7" format, 11px, right of the bar
Present on every slide EXCEPT the last (CTA)
Position: absolute right edge, full height, 44px wide
Present on every slide EXCEPT slide 1 (cover)
// comments — always on their own line
Comments ALWAYS go on their own line, never inline with code on the same line (caused formatting bugs in previous versions)
.c { font-family: JetBrains Mono; font-size: 11px; color: #F97316; background: rgba(249,115,22,0.1); padding: 1px 5px; border-radius: 4px; }
Applied to: every method, function, property, CSS class, or code concept mentioned in prose — e.g.
background: rgba(249,115,22,0.06); border-left: 3px solid #F97316; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 12px;
Contains: 1–2 sentence plain-English explanation of the code, with inline code spans liberally applied
.frd { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 12px; padding: 9px 0; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.09); }
.fid { width: 32px; height: 32px; border-radius: 8px; background: rgba(253,186,116,0.12); flex-shrink: 0; }
.sr { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 12px; padding: 10px 0; border-bottom: 1px solid #E8E6E0; }
.sn { font-family: Bricolage Grotesque; font-size: 22px; font-weight: 300; color: #F97316; min-width: 28px; }
Heading: "[N] days in. Still building." — 34px Bricolage Grotesque
background: var(--lbg); color: var(--bd); padding: 11px 24px; border-radius: 28px; font-weight: 700;
NO swipe arrow. Progress bar at 100% full.
Day 40: Building a Google Keep Clone
Google Keep clone — Vanilla JS, OOP (Note + App classes), full DOM manipulation
to close modal on outside click
Body-level event delegation — one listener on
Google Keep clone upgraded — notes now survive page refresh
Same repo as Day 40 — app.js was the main file updated
in one call, invoked after every mutation
state property to track which note is being edited in the modal
Graceful Modal Closure — open/close via separate buttons,
Day 42: Completing the JS Google Keep Clone + Starting React
Milestone post — vanilla JS era complete, React era begins
JS Google Keep Clone (completed) + React Google Keep (started)
6-slide terminal aesthetic — diverges from orange brand system
JS Keep Clone — what was completed:
Full vanilla JS + OOP Google Keep clone with persistent notes
localStorage persistence from Day 41 carried forward
Visual update slide showing the running app screenshot
Began rebuilding the Google Keep clone in React + Vite + JSX
npm run dev workflow, component structure started
Progress: app scaffolded, components folder created
8. Workflow — How to Create a New Carousel
Course lesson screenshots (what topics/lessons were covered that day)
Code files (GitHub link or pasted directly — app.js, challenges, etc.)
Any specific framing preferences or angles he wants to highlight
Analyse all material. Ask: what is the most interesting or teachable concept from today? Frame that as the carousel theme. Options:
Single project deep-dive — "Building X" with key concepts shown via code blocks
Concept explainer — "How [X] works" / "N Ways to [do X]"
Data flow / mental model — numbered steps showing a process
Before/after — what changed, what was added, why it matters
Always turn learning into teaching. The reader should gain something from each slide, not just watch a learning log.
Produce a complete standalone HTML file using the Day 41 file as the template. Key checklist:
Fonts imported from Google Fonts (Bricolage Grotesque, DM Sans, JetBrains Mono)
Sizer div pattern used (NOT aspect-ratio CSS) — see Section 4.2
All image slots use dashed placeholder divs with
Footer on every slide except slide 1 with correct day number
Progress bar percentages correct for total slide count
Swipe arrow absent from last slide
Instructions box at top of page body
Download button label: "Download all [N] slides as PNG"
Wait 2–3 seconds for Google Fonts to load
Click orange download button — all slides export automatically
Profile pic: Instagram photo URL or local image
JS logo: can use uploaded file or public CDN URL
Upload 7 PNG files to Instagram as a carousel post
Use the suggested caption from Claude
aspect-ratio CSS causes subpixel bleed in html2canvas
Use padding-top: 125% sizer div pattern. Capture .slide-inner not .vp. Hard-crop output canvas.
Fonts look wrong in downloaded PNG
Google Fonts not loaded before capture
Wait 2–3 seconds after opening file. Fonts must load before clicking download.
Code block comments on wrong line
Inline comments after code share the .cl span
Comments ALWAYS get their own separate <span class="cl"> line. Never mix comment and code on same line.
CDN URL has expiry token (oe=... parameter)
Re-fetch URL from Instagram or host image on stable CDN. Placeholder is safe default.
Some browsers require user gesture for each download
300ms delay between downloads handles this in most cases. Chrome works best.
Gradient slide background (orange gradient, CTA only)
Section label tag — dark slide (light orange)
Slide heading — light / dark / gradient variant
Inline code span — light slides (orange on cream bg)
Inline code span — dark slides (light orange on dark bg)
Feature row — dark slide (icon box + title + description)
Step row — light slide (number + title + description)
Slide footer — flex row, handle left, day badge right
Code block — container + header + filename label
CTA logo lockup — row + circle + name
Dot navigation — row + dot + active dot

Day 42 marks a deliberate divergence from the orange brand system. The terminal aesthetic is a separate design language used for milestone and project-completion carousels. Both systems coexist — the orange brand remains for standard daily-log posts.
#00FF41 — Matrix green. Main accent: prompts, borders, highlights
#00B82F — Darker green for light slide borders
rgba(255,255,255,0.35) — Muted text on dark slides
rgba(0,255,65,0.18) — Green border on dark slides
#007A20 — Green used on light slides (prompts, tags)
#141210 — Near-black for body text on light slides
JetBrains Mono exclusively throughout. No Bricolage Grotesque, no DM Sans. This reinforces the terminal / hacker aesthetic.
Cover — class='slide' (dark). React atom hero right side, profile, taglines, big heading
JS Keep Visual — class='slide lt' (light). App screenshot
JS Keep Features — class='slide' (dark). Feature list with terminal-style rows
React Progress — class='slide lt rlt' (light, React variant). Step list
CTA — class='slide cta-bg' (gradient). Status, handle, follow card
Modifier classes override colors contextually rather than duplicating full CSS per slide:
Light slide — #F5F5EF bg, dark text, green accents
React light variant — overrides accents to React cyan (#0284c7)
Gradient CTA background (radial green glow from bottom)
Slide padding container (flex column, full height, 30px padding)
Status tag — 10px, green, letter-spacing, ALL CAPS
Profile photo row on cover slide
Large tagline lines on cover ([ #100DAYSOFCODE ] style)
Comment line — muted color, // prefix style
Info bar at bottom of slide (repo link etc.)
Uses a clone-to-offscreen pattern, different from the Day 41 navigate-to-slide approach:

prepareCloneForExport(slide) clones the slide, injects animation: none important style to freeze all animations, and handles the .cta-bg gradient (hardcoded string — getComputedStyle returns rgba(0,0,0,0) for gradient elements)
React atom and CTA gradient are pre-rasterised on page load: drawn on offscreen canvases, converted to PNG data URLs, injected as img elements — so cloneNode copies them perfectly
Individual buttons ([01]-[06]) plus a download-all button
backgroundColor: null in html2canvas — background comes from the wrap div style
All images embedded as base64 data URIs — file is fully self-contained
Profile photo embedded as base64 JPEG inside the HTML
React atom logo drawn via Canvas 2D API on load, never stored as external file
CTA gradient drawn via Canvas 2D API on load, injected as absolutely-positioned img behind slide content
11.7 Key Bug Fixes Discovered in Day 42
Code placed inside script tag with src attribute — browsers ignore inline body when src is set. Fix: two separate script tags
html2canvas cannot reliably capture cloned SVG. Fix: draw on offscreen canvas, convert to PNG data URL, inject as img on page load
getComputedStyle().backgroundColor returns transparent for gradient elements. Fix: hardcode gradient string for .cta-bg in prepareCloneForExport
background:#000 on video element shows as black padding. Fix: remove background property from video tag
Bash heredoc escapes ! in some shells. Fix: Python byte-level replacement — data.replace(0x5c 0x21, 0x21)
Report v3 — @uncledullaz Instagram Carousel System. Updated with Day 42 terminal aesthetic.
